event_name: Judge The Book By Its Cover
description_html:
Description:

  • Every story makes its first impression before a single page is turned.
  • In this event, participants are presented with a curated mix of book and manga covers and challenged to judge the story hidden within them.
  • With minimal to no summaries or background information provided, participants must rely entirely on visual cues. Your task is to write a short, powerful judgment based only on the cover—capturing the genre, emotions, and the world it hints at. Each decision is instinctive, and each word must carry weight.
  • This event tests perception, imagination, and expressive clarity. The most compelling entries are those that transform a silent image into a vivid narrative.

Rulebook:

Event Duration:

  • 2 Hours

Prize Pool:

  • ₹1,500

Team Participation:

  • Team size: 1–2 members
  • On-spot registration is allowed before the event begins

Event Structure:

  • The event consists of multiple rounds
  • Each round lasts 10 minutes
  • In every round, teams will be shown one book or manga cover

Task:

  • For each round, teams must write a short judgment based only on the displayed cover
  • Judgments should reflect:
  • Interpretation
  • Imagination
  • Attention to visual details
  • Submissions must be completed within the 10-minute time limit

Scoring & Evaluation:

  • Each round will be scored independently
  • Scores will accumulate across all rounds

Winning Criteria:

  • The team with the highest total score at the end of the event will be declared the winner
  • In case of a tie, a tiebreaker round may be conducted

Rules & Conduct:

  • Use of external resources is strictly prohibited
  • Plagiarism or copying will result in disqualification
  • Teams must strictly adhere to time limits

Prizes:

  • Total prize pool: ₹1,500
  • Prize distribution will be announced at the end of the event

General Guidelines:

  • Participants must maintain decorum and fair play
  • Judges’ decisions are final
  • Organizers reserve the right to make changes if necessary
